rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee in component type annexes; use assign only in implementation annexes for output features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model scope—never invent or copy external names.
generic_rules: Express constraints using only inputs, outputs, and constants explicitly visible in the component scope.
Type-level annexes should relate visible outputs to visible inputs; implementation annexes should bind outputs only.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` to wrap all AGREE clauses.
Declare constants with `const name : type = value;` and equations with `eq name : type = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables—they are not in scope unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Never place guarantee clauses inside implementation annexes.
